title: dryopteris abortiva
intro: Dryopteris abortiva, also known as American goldenseal or goldenseal, is a perennial plant native to the eastern United States and Canada, belonging to the fern family (Dryopteridaceae). The medicinal properties of goldenseal lie in its roots, which contain the alkaloids berberine, hydrastine, and hydrastinine, known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of goldenseal include its ability to treat respiratory tract infections, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Goldenseal is commonly prepared in herbal supplements, teas, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs, such as echinacea and ginseng, to enhance its medicinal effects.
